campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on September 2, 2025, to review the city's finances for July, including how the city's investments performed that month and a detailed treasurer's report on the city's money. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, including walking, biking, and public transit.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
Multimodal transportation improvements (walking, biking, transit options)
Implementation workplan for Campbell's transportation plan

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing a new ordinance and skipped the usual first reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance will be voted on and finalized at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve official records from their previous meetings in March and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting focused on administrative tasks like confirming the accuracy of past meeting notes and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six March 2026 meetings (regular, special, and closed sessions)
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute contract details

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for late April. They reviewed what items should be added to their April 21st and April 28th meeting schedules, decided which topics needed specific time slots, and checked that all required public notices were properly filed.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 21, 2026 meeting agenda with any new items
Organized the April 28, 2026 draft agenda and added new business
Set time limits for specific agenda items to keep meetings on track
Verified public notice requirements were met for all agenda items
Reviewed public records from April 2-9, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's city council met to review the budget and spending plans for the next fiscal year (2026-27), focusing on what capital projects the city will invest in. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers.
keyTopics: 2026-27 capital improvement budget and planned city projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review their previous meeting notes from March and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would amend the city's residential tenant protections program, though specific details of what protections would change weren't outlined in this agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
Changes to tenant protection rules (Chapter 19.71 of municipal code)
New residential tenant protections ordinance being introduced
Housing and Human Services Commission business

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where residents were invited to speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The council also discussed electing a new Chair and Vice Chair for leadership positions, and was preparing to review budget plans for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents can address the council on any city matter
Chair and Vice Chair elections for council leadership
Fiscal Year budget presentation and input
3-minute speaker limit for public comments

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 covered important legal rules that residents need to know. If you want to challenge any major city decision in court, you have 90 days from when that decision is made to file a lawsuit, or you'll lose your right to challenge it.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over major decisions
Legal challenge rules for quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure requirements
How residents can protect their legal rights

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative items, including updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2026, reviewing new project requests from individual council members, and potentially changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled. The council also approved meeting minutes and reviewed the schedule for upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 5-year federal funding and housing plan
New project requests submitted by individual councilmembers
Possible changes to how city council vacancies are filled
Approval of previous meeting minutes and council meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a proposal to upgrade the historic status of a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is requesting permission to add 5,000 square feet of additional floor space in exchange for renovating the building to improve its historic and earthquake safety features.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade (Category 4 to Category 2)
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown Commercial zoning district (Pedestrian Shopping area)
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and minutes approval
